author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Full M365 Agents Toolkit provisioning of the Discovery Gap Sentinel declarative agent, resolving a chain of four cascading errors. Starting from a broken m365agents.yml (wrong schema), working through a doubled path bug in plugin JSON spec URLs, a toolkit path resolution inconsistency between the zip builder and validator, and a missing deploy lifecycle stage. All four were resolved; the app (f6d6a2eb-5b09-43c5-b491-766603695e76) provisioned with 57/57 validations passing. Additionally: fixed githubActions.yaml to restore context_notes optional input and correct path descriptions to repo-relative only; confirmed repo OAuth scope is sufficient for workflow_dispatch; added an adaptive card to the uploadInputFile function showing filename and a View in SharePoint button; updated the Teams Dev Portal OAuth connection to reference the new provisioned app ID.
problems: m365agents.yml had wrong schema format (custom fields like name/projectType/auth) instead of the lifecycle YAML format the toolkit requires
Plugin JSON spec URLs used 'actions/xxx.yaml' relative to files already inside actions/ folder, causing doubled path 'actions/actions/xxx.yaml' during zip build
After fixing spec URLs to 'xxx.yaml', zip built but validator could not find the YAML files — toolkit zip builder and validator use different path resolution bases
m365agents.yml had no deploy lifecycle stage, causing error when Deploy button was clicked
githubActions.yaml had removed context_notes from createWorkflowDispatch inputs, but instructions.txt still told the agent to gather it
githubActions.yaml described brief_path/transcript_path as supporting SharePoint paths, but the workflow only accepts repo-relative paths
workflow scope was removed from GitHub OAuth spec, unnecessarily (repo scope is sufficient for workflow_dispatch)
approaches: Replaced m365agents.yml content with correct v1.11 lifecycle schema (version: v1.11, environmentFolderPath, provision/publish stages)
Changed plugin JSON spec URLs from 'actions/xxx.yaml' to 'xxx.yaml' (relative to plugin JSON file location in actions/)
Moved plugin JSONs (sharepointActions.json, githubActions.json) to appPackage root so zip builder and validator both resolve 'actions/xxx.yaml' correctly
Updated declarativeAgent.json to reference root-level plugin files without path prefix
Restored context_notes as optional input in githubActions.yaml createWorkflowDispatch
Reverted path descriptions to repo-relative only
Confirmed repo scope is sufficient; reverted workflow scope addition
Added response_semantics adaptive card to uploadInputFile in both sharepointActions.json copies
results: App f6d6a2eb-5b09-43c5-b491-766603695e76 provisioned via M365 Agents Toolkit 6.10.0
deploy lifecycle executes with 0 steps cleanly
User updated Teams Dev Portal OAuth connection (GitHub Discovery Gap Sentinel) to allow new app ID f6d6a2eb
uploadInputFile now renders an adaptive card with filename and View in SharePoint button
context_notes optional input restored to workflow dispatch spec
Brief/transcript paths correctly described as repo-relative only

decisions.decision: Plugin JSONs live at appPackage root, not in actions/ subdirectory — YAML specs stay in actions/
repo OAuth scope is sufficient for GitHub workflow_dispatch — workflow scope not needed
Provision via M365 Agents Toolkit, not manual sideload zip, for the registered app going forward

open_questions: Has end-to-end workflow dispatch been tested from the agent (Teams → GitHub → pipeline)?
Does the SharePoint uploadInputFile OAuth work correctly with the new app ID f6d6a2eb?
Are the changes on branch fix/apppackage-v0.2.0-sync committed and pushed?
